content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Changed spawn_reap_with_timeout_drops_on_expiry from #[tokio::test] to #[tokio::test(start_paused = true)] so that tokio::time::timeout and tokio::time::sleep advance via simulated time rather than wall-clock. This eliminates timing-dependent flakiness under CI load. The test now completes in 0.00s (previously 0.30s), confirming paused time is active.

## Steps

0. [Completed] Identify the test attribute for spawn_reap_with_timeout_drops_on_expiry at line 4160
1. [Completed] Change #[tokio::test] to #[tokio::test(start_paused = true)] — safe because this test uses only tokio timers and std::future::pending(), no real processes
2. [Completed] Verify test passes with paused time: 1 passed in 0.00s (previously 0.30s)
3. [Completed] Verify full test suite: 585 unit + 756 integration tests passed, 0 failed
4. [Completed] Verify cargo fmt --check passes

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test spawn_reap_with_timeout_drops: 1 passed in 0.00s (paused time confirmed active)
- cargo test: 585 unit + 756 integration tests passed, 0 failed
- cargo fmt --check: clean

## Outstanding Risks

- On platforms where SIGKILL is truly ineffective (uninterruptible I/O 'D' state), the process remains as a zombie after handle drop — inherent to the OS; the timeout correctly avoids blocking the tokio runtime
- Pre-existing clippy warnings (16 in other files) remain unfixed — not introduced by this change
